title: Introduction to htaBIM
text: htaBIM provides structured, reproducible framework budget impact modelling (BIM) health technology assessment (HTA), following ISPOR Task Force guidelines (Sullivan et al., 2014; Mauskopf et al., 2007). budget impact model answers: “new treatment reimbursed, financial impact payer’s budget next 1–5 years?”

title: Introduction to htaBIM
code: bim_population() -> bim_market_share() -> bim_costs() -> bim_model() -> outputs

title: Introduction to htaBIM
text: epidemiology funnel estimates number patients eligible treatment year reference population size cascade rates.
code: pop <- bim_population(   indication     = "Disease X",   country        = "custom",   years          = 1:5,   prevalence     = 0.003,   n_total_pop    = 42e6,   diagnosed_rate = 0.60,   treated_rate   = 0.45,   eligible_rate  = 0.30,   growth_rate    = 0.005,   data_source    = "Illustrative values only" ) summary(pop) #>  #> == Population Summary == #> Indication   : Disease X #> Country      : custom #> Approach     : prevalent #>  #> Epidemiological funnel (Year 1): #>   Total pop          : 4.2e+07 #>   Prevalent/incident : 126,000 #>   Diagnosed          : 75,600 #>   Treated            : 34,020 #>   Eligible           : 10,206 #>  #> Data source  : Illustrative values only

title: Introduction to htaBIM
text: Define mix treatments current world (without new drug) new world (new drug), plus scenario variants.
code: ms <- bim_market_share(   population     = pop,   treatments     = c("Drug C (SoC)", "Drug B", "Drug A (new)"),   new_drug       = "Drug A (new)",   shares_current = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 0.75, "Drug B" = 0.25, "Drug A (new)" = 0.00),   shares_new     = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 0.60, "Drug B" = 0.20, "Drug A (new)" = 0.20),   dynamics       = "linear",   uptake_params  = list(ramp_years = 3),   scenarios = list(     conservative = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 0.68, "Drug B" = 0.22, "Drug A (new)" = 0.10),     optimistic   = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 0.50, "Drug B" = 0.18, "Drug A (new)" = 0.32)   ) ) print(ms) #>  #> -- htaBIM Market Share -- #>  #> Treatments : Drug C (SoC), Drug B, Drug A (new)  #> New drug   : Drug A (new)  #> Dynamics   : linear  #> Scenarios  : current, base, conservative, optimistic  #>  #> Year 1 shares (base, with new drug): #>   Drug C (SoC)              : 70.0% #>   Drug B                    : 23.3% #>   Drug A (new)              : 6.7%

title: Introduction to htaBIM
text: Per-patient annual costs built treatment cost category (drug, admin, monitoring, adverse events, ). Adverse event costs can computed event-rate table.
code: ae_tab <- data.frame(   ae_name   = c("Injection site reaction", "Fatigue"),   rate      = c(0.07, 0.12),   unit_cost = c(180, 95) ) ae_new <- bim_costs_ae("Drug A (new)", ae_tab)  costs <- bim_costs(   treatments       = c("Drug C (SoC)", "Drug B", "Drug A (new)"),   currency         = "GBP",   price_year       = 2025L,   drug_costs       = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 220,   "Drug B" = 22400, "Drug A (new)" = 28800),   admin_costs      = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 0,     "Drug B" = 0,     "Drug A (new)" = 480),   monitoring_costs = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 650,   "Drug B" = 1550,  "Drug A (new)" = 1950),   ae_costs         = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 80,    "Drug B" = 210,   "Drug A (new)" = as.numeric(ae_new)) ) print(costs) #>  #> -- htaBIM Costs -- #>  #> Currency   : GBP (2025 prices) #> Treatments : Drug C (SoC), Drug B, Drug A (new) #>  #> Total annual cost per patient (Year 1): #>   Drug A (new)              : GBP 31,254 #>   Drug B                    : GBP 24,160 #>   Drug C (SoC)              : GBP 950

title: Introduction to htaBIM
text: DSA tornado diagram (Year 5)
code: sens <- bim_sensitivity_spec(   prevalence_range           = c(0.0015, 0.005),   eligible_rate_range        = c(0.20, 0.45),   drug_cost_multiplier_range = c(0.85, 1.15) ) dsa <- bim_run_dsa(model, sens, year = 5L) bim_plot_tornado(dsa, currency = "GBP")

title: Introduction to htaBIM
text: PSA samples uncertain parameters simultaneously statistical distributions (Beta rates, LogNormal costs) produce distribution budget impact outcomes. PSA distribution Year 5 budget impact
code: set.seed(42) psa <- bim_run_psa(   model,   n_sim            = 200L,   prevalence_se    = 0.0005,   eligible_rate_se = 0.05,   cost_cv          = 0.10,   year             = 5L ) print(psa) #> htaBIM Probabilistic Sensitivity Analysis #>   Year: 5 | Scenario: base | Simulations: 200 / 200 converged #>   Base-case budget impact: GBP 50,999,568 #>  #>   PSA summary (budget impact): #>     Mean:   GBP 49,662,228 #>     Median: GBP 47,714,422 #>     SD:     GBP 12,903,021 #>     95% CrI: GBP 29,882,477  to  GBP 78,616,621 bim_plot_psa(psa)

title: htaBIM — Budget Impact Modelling for HTA
text: htaBIM provides complete, reproducible framework budget impact modelling (BIM) health technology assessment (HTA), following ISPOR Task Force guidelines. replaces error-prone Excel-based BIM workbooks structured, auditable R workflows produce submission-quality outputs NICE, CADTH, EU-HTA dossiers.

title: htaBIM — Budget Impact Modelling for HTA
text: 📊 Epidemiology-driven population estimation — prevalent, incident, combined approaches eligibility funnel 📈 Flexible market share modelling — constant, linear ramp, logistic S-curve, step uptake dynamics 💊 Multi-category cost inputs — drug, administration, monitoring, adverse events, rebate support 🔄 Multi-year projections — annual cumulative budget impact across scenarios 🏥 Pre-built payer perspectives — NHS England, CADTH, US commercial, custom 📉 Deterministic sensitivity analysis — DSA tornado diagrams 📋 Submission-ready outputs — formatted tables, plots, text/HTML reports 🖥️ Interactive Shiny dashboard — launch_shiny() stakeholder communication

title: htaBIM — Budget Impact Modelling for HTA
code: # From CRAN install.packages("htaBIM")  # Development version from GitHub # install.packages("pak") pak::pkg_install("Heorlytics/htaBIM")

title: htaBIM — Budget Impact Modelling for HTA
code: library(htaBIM)  # Step 1: Define eligible population pop <- bim_population(   indication     = "IgA Nephropathy",   country        = "custom",   years          = 1:5,   prevalence     = 0.003,   n_total_pop    = 42e6,   diagnosed_rate = 0.60,   treated_rate   = 0.45,   eligible_rate  = 0.30 )  # Step 2: Define market shares ms <- bim_market_share(   population     = pop,   treatments     = c("Standard of Care", "New Drug"),   new_drug       = "New Drug",   shares_current = c("Standard of Care" = 1.0, "New Drug" = 0.0),   shares_new     = c("Standard of Care" = 0.8, "New Drug" = 0.2),   dynamics       = "linear",   uptake_params  = list(ramp_years = 3) )  # Step 3: Define costs costs <- bim_costs(   treatments = c("Standard of Care", "New Drug"),   currency   = "GBP",   drug_costs = c("Standard of Care" = 1500, "New Drug" = 28000) )  # Step 4: Assemble and run model <- bim_model(pop, ms, costs, payer = bim_payer_nhs()) summary(model)  # Step 5: Visualise and report plot(model, type = "line") plot(model, type = "bar") bim_report(model)

title: Per-patient cost breakdown by component and treatment — bim_cost_breakdown
text: model bim_model object. year integer(1). Price year extract costs . Defaults model$costs$meta$price_year (base price year, inflation). currency_millions logical(1). Express values millions. Default FALSE (per-patient costs typically whole currency units). digits integer(1). Decimal places. Default 0L.

title: Per-patient cost breakdown by component and treatment — bim_cost_breakdown
text: data.frame rows = cost categories columns = treatments, plus Total row. Values formatted character strings. Carries "caption" attribute.

title: Per-patient cost breakdown by component and treatment — bim_cost_breakdown
code: pop <- bim_population(   indication  = "Disease X", country = "custom",   years = 1:5, prevalence = 0.003, n_total_pop = 42e6,   diagnosed_rate = 0.60, treated_rate = 0.45, eligible_rate = 0.30 ) ms <- bim_market_share(   population     = pop,   treatments     = c("Drug C (SoC)", "Drug A (new)"),   new_drug       = "Drug A (new)",   shares_current = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 1.0, "Drug A (new)" = 0.0),   shares_new     = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 0.8, "Drug A (new)" = 0.2) ) costs <- bim_costs(   treatments       = c("Drug C (SoC)", "Drug A (new)"),   drug_costs       = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 500,  "Drug A (new)" = 25000),   monitoring_costs = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 200,  "Drug A (new)" = 1500),   ae_costs         = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 50,   "Drug A (new)" = 300) ) model <- bim_model(pop, ms, costs) bim_cost_breakdown(model) #>        Cost component Drug C (SoC) Drug A (new) #> 1           Drug cost          500       25,000 #> 2 Administration cost            0            0 #> 3     Monitoring cost          200        1,500 #> 4  Adverse event cost           50          300 #> 5          Other cost            0            0 #> 6   Total per patient          750       26,800

what: Build per-patient annual cost inputs for a budget impact model — bim_costs
title: Build per-patient annual cost inputs for a budget impact model — bim_costs
text: Constructs per-patient annual cost structure treatment cost category (drug, administration, monitoring, adverse events, ). Supports optional inflation adjustment, discounting, confidential rebates.

title: Build per-patient annual cost inputs for a budget impact model — bim_costs
code: bim_costs(   treatments,   years = 1:5,   drug_costs = NULL,   admin_costs = NULL,   monitoring_costs = NULL,   ae_costs = NULL,   other_costs = NULL,   currency = "GBP",   price_year = as.integer(format(Sys.Date(), "%Y")),   inflation_rate = 0,   rebates = NULL )

title: Build per-patient annual cost inputs for a budget impact model — bim_costs
text: treatments character. Vector treatment names. Must match bim_market_share(). years integer. Projection years (default 1:5). drug_costs Named numeric vector NULL. Annual drug cost per patient treatment. admin_costs Named numeric vector NULL. Annual administration cost per patient (infusion, injection nurse, etc.). monitoring_costs Named numeric vector NULL. Annual monitoring costs (lab tests, clinic visits, imaging). ae_costs Named numeric vector NULL. Annual adverse event management costs per patient. other_costs Named numeric vector NULL. direct medical costs captured . currency character(1). ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. "GBP", "USD", "EUR", "CAD"). Default "GBP". price_year integer(1). Reference price year. Default current calendar year. inflation_rate numeric(1). Annual inflation rate applied non-drug costs years beyond Year 1. Default 0.0. rebates Named numeric vector NULL. Confidential rebates proportions (e.g. c(DrugA = 0.15) 15% rebate). Applied drug_costs kept internal (printed default).

title: Build per-patient annual cost inputs for a budget impact model — bim_costs
text: object class bim_costs, list containing: costs data.frame columns treatment, year, category, unit_cost, total_annual_cost. total data.frame treatment, year, total_cost_per_patient. params List input parameters (rebates stored printed). meta List currency, price_year, treatments.

title: Build per-patient annual cost inputs for a budget impact model — bim_costs
code: costs <- bim_costs(   treatments = c("RASi", "Sparsentan", "Sibeprenlimab"),   currency   = "GBP",   price_year = 2025L,   drug_costs = c(     RASi          = 200,     Sparsentan    = 22000,     Sibeprenlimab = 28500   ),   monitoring_costs = c(     RASi          = 650,     Sparsentan    = 1500,     Sibeprenlimab = 1900   ) ) print(costs) #>  #> -- htaBIM Costs -- #>  #> Currency   : GBP (2025 prices) #> Treatments : RASi, Sparsentan, Sibeprenlimab #>  #> Total annual cost per patient (Year 1): #>   RASi                      : GBP 850 #>   Sibeprenlimab             : GBP 30,400 #>   Sparsentan                : GBP 23,500

what: Specify treatment market shares for a budget impact model — bim_market_share
title: Specify treatment market shares for a budget impact model — bim_market_share
text: Defines treatment market shares evolve time, current scenario (without new drug) new scenario (new drug introduced). Supports constant, linear ramp, logistic S-curve, step uptake dynamics.

title: Specify treatment market shares for a budget impact model — bim_market_share
code: bim_market_share(   population,   treatments,   new_drug,   shares_current,   shares_new,   dynamics = c("constant", "linear", "logistic", "step"),   uptake_params = NULL,   scenarios = NULL )

title: Specify treatment market shares for a budget impact model — bim_market_share
text: population bim_population object bim_population(). treatments character. Vector treatment names, including new drug. new_drug character(1). Name new intervention. Must element treatments. shares_current Named numeric vector. Market shares current scenario (without new drug). Values must sum 1 [0, 1]. Names must match treatments. shares_new Named numeric vector. Market shares new scenario (new drug full uptake). Values must sum 1. Names must match treatments. dynamics character(1). new drug's uptake evolves: "constant" – shares_new apply uniformly years (default). "linear" – new drug ramps linearly 0 target share uptake_params$ramp_years years. "logistic" – S-curve uptake. Requires uptake_params$year_50pct optionally uptake_params$steepness. "step" – shares_new named list one vector per year. uptake_params list NULL. Parameters controlling uptake dynamics: "linear": list(ramp_years = 3). "logistic": list(year_50pct = 2, steepness = 2). scenarios Named list NULL. Alternative market share vectors (named numerics, structure shares_new) scenario analysis. E.g. list(conservative = c(...), optimistic = c(...)).

title: Specify treatment market shares for a budget impact model — bim_market_share
text: object class bim_market_share, list containing: shares data.frame columns year, treatment, scenario, share, n_patients. params List input parameters. meta List treatments, new_drug, dynamics.

what: Assemble and run a budget impact model — bim_model
title: Assemble and run a budget impact model — bim_model
text: Combines bim_population(), bim_market_share(), bim_costs() object complete budget impact model computes annual cumulative budget impact across scenarios. budget impact year t defined : $$BI_t = \sum_i N_t \cdot s_i^{new}(t) \cdot c_i(t)             - \sum_i N_t \cdot s_i^{current}(t) \cdot c_i(t)$$ \(N_t\) number eligible patients, \(s_i(t)\) market share treatment \(\), \(c_i(t)\) cost per patient treatment \(\).

title: Assemble and run a budget impact model — bim_model
code: bim_model(   population,   market_share,   costs,   payer = bim_payer_default(),   discount_rate = 0,   label = NULL )

title: Assemble and run a budget impact model — bim_model
text: population bim_population object bim_population(). market_share bim_market_share object bim_market_share(). costs bim_costs object bim_costs(). payer bim_payer object bim_payer() one pre-built payer functions. Default bim_payer_default(). discount_rate numeric(1). Annual discount rate applied Year 2+ costs. Per ISPOR guidelines, base case undiscounted (0). Default 0. label character(1) NULL. Optional model label reporting.

title: Assemble and run a budget impact model — bim_model
text: object class bim_model, list containing: population input bim_population object. market_share input bim_market_share object. costs input bim_costs object. payer input bim_payer object. results list annual cumulative data frames. meta list model metadata.

title: Assemble and run a budget impact model — bim_model
code: pop <- bim_population(   indication    = "IgA Nephropathy",   country       = "GB",   years         = 1:3,   prevalence    = 0.003,   n_total_pop   = 42e6,   eligible_rate = 0.30 )  ms <- bim_market_share(   population     = pop,   treatments     = c("RASi", "Sparsentan", "Sibeprenlimab"),   new_drug       = "Sibeprenlimab",   shares_current = c(RASi = 0.75, Sparsentan = 0.25, Sibeprenlimab = 0.00),   shares_new     = c(RASi = 0.60, Sparsentan = 0.20, Sibeprenlimab = 0.20) )  costs <- bim_costs(   treatments = c("RASi", "Sparsentan", "Sibeprenlimab"),   drug_costs = c(RASi = 200, Sparsentan = 22000, Sibeprenlimab = 28500) )  model <- bim_model(pop, ms, costs, label = "IgAN BIM") summary(model) #>  #> == htaBIM Model Summary == #> ======================================================= #> Label      : IgAN BIM #> Indication : IgA Nephropathy #> Country    : GB #> Currency   : GBP (2026 prices) #> New drug   : Sibeprenlimab #> Payer      : Healthcare system (default) #> Discount   : 0.0% #> ------------------------------------------------------- #> Scenario: BASE #> Year    Budget (curr)    Budget (new)     Impact          #> 1       GBP 213,570,000  GBP 386,316,000  GBP 172,746,000 #> 2       GBP 213,570,000  GBP 386,316,000  GBP 172,746,000 #> 3       GBP 213,570,000  GBP 386,316,000  GBP 172,746,000 #>  #> Cumulative impact (3 yrs): GBP 518,238,000 #>

what: Estimate the annual eligible patient population for a budget impact model — bim_population
title: Estimate the annual eligible patient population for a budget impact model — bim_population
text: Builds year--year estimate number patients eligible new treatment, using epidemiology-driven funnel approach aligned ISPOR Task Force guidelines (Sullivan et al., 2014). Supports prevalent, incident, combined population approaches.

title: Estimate the annual eligible patient population for a budget impact model — bim_population
code: bim_population(   indication,   country = "GB",   years = 1:5,   prevalence = NULL,   incidence = NULL,   n_total_pop = NULL,   diagnosed_rate = 1,   treated_rate = 1,   eligible_rate = 1,   growth_rate = 0,   approach = c("prevalent", "incident", "both"),   data_source = NULL )

title: Estimate the annual eligible patient population for a budget impact model — bim_population
text: indication character(1). Name disease indication. Used outputs reports. country character(1). ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g. "GB", "US", "CA", "DE"). Used look built-population data n_total_pop NULL. Use "custom" rely solely n_total_pop. years integer. Vector projection years (e.g. 1:5). Default 1:5. prevalence numeric(1) NULL. Point prevalence proportion (e.g. 0.002 0.2%). Required approach "prevalent" "". incidence numeric(1) NULL. Annual incidence rate per 100,000. Required approach "incident" "". n_total_pop numeric(1) NULL. Total reference population size. NULL country recognised, uses built-population data. diagnosed_rate numeric(1). Proportion prevalent/incident cases diagnosed. Must [0, 1]. Default 1.0. treated_rate numeric(1). Proportion diagnosed patients receiving systemic treatment. Must [0, 1]. Default 1.0. eligible_rate numeric(1). Proportion treated patients eligible new drug (e.g. meeting label criteria). Must [0, 1]. Default 1.0. growth_rate numeric(1). Annual growth rate applied total population (e.g. 0.005 0.5% per year). Default 0.0. approach character(1). Population approach: "prevalent" (stock population), "incident" (new cases per year), "" (sum prevalent incident). Default "prevalent". data_source character(1) NULL. Citation epidemiology data, appended outputs. Optional.

title: Estimate the annual eligible patient population for a budget impact model — bim_population
text: object class bim_population, list containing: annual data.frame columns year, n_total_pop, n_prevalent_or_incident, n_diagnosed, n_treated, n_eligible. params list input parameters. meta list indication, country, approach, data_source.

title: Estimate the annual eligible patient population for a budget impact model — bim_population

title: Run a deterministic sensitivity analysis on a budget impact model — bim_run_dsa
code: pop <- bim_population(   indication    = "Example",   country       = "GB",   years         = 1:3,   prevalence    = 0.003,   n_total_pop   = 42e6,   eligible_rate = 0.30 ) ms <- bim_market_share(   population     = pop,   treatments     = c("RASi", "NewDrug"),   new_drug       = "NewDrug",   shares_current = c(RASi = 1.0, NewDrug = 0.0),   shares_new     = c(RASi = 0.8, NewDrug = 0.2) ) costs <- bim_costs(   treatments = c("RASi", "NewDrug"),   drug_costs = c(RASi = 500, NewDrug = 25000) ) model <- bim_model(pop, ms, costs)  sens <- bim_sensitivity_spec(   prevalence_range        = c(0.002, 0.005),   eligible_rate_range     = c(0.20, 0.45),   drug_cost_multiplier_range = c(0.85, 1.15) ) dsa <- bim_run_dsa(model, sens, year = 3L) print(dsa) #>  #> -- htaBIM DSA Results -- #>  #> Parameter                       BI (low)      BI (base)     BI (high)    #> ------------------------------------------------------------------------ #> New drug cost (multiplier)      156,870,000   185,220,000   213,570,000  #> Prevalence                      185,220,000   185,220,000   185,220,000  #> Eligible rate                   185,220,000   185,220,000   185,220,000

what: Run a probabilistic sensitivity analysis (PSA) — bim_run_psa
title: Run a probabilistic sensitivity analysis (PSA) — bim_run_psa
text: Performs Monte Carlo PSA repeatedly sampling uncertain parameters assumed statistical distributions re-running budget impact model draw. produces distribution budget impact outcomes reflects joint parameter uncertainty. Distributional assumptions Prevalence, diagnosed rate, treated rate, eligible rate — Beta distribution parameterised base-case value standard error. Drug cost — LogNormal distribution parameterised base-case value coefficient variation (CV).

title: Run a probabilistic sensitivity analysis (PSA) — bim_run_psa
code: bim_run_psa(   model,   n_sim = 1000L,   prevalence_se = NULL,   diagnosed_rate_se = NULL,   treated_rate_se = NULL,   eligible_rate_se = NULL,   cost_cv = NULL,   year = NULL,   scenario = "base",   seed = NULL )

title: Run a probabilistic sensitivity analysis (PSA) — bim_run_psa
text: model bim_model object (base case). n_sim integer(1). Number Monte Carlo simulations. Default 1000L. prevalence_se numeric(1). Standard error prevalence. NULL (default), prevalence held fixed. diagnosed_rate_se numeric(1) NULL. SE diagnosed rate. treated_rate_se numeric(1) NULL. SE treated rate. eligible_rate_se numeric(1) NULL. SE eligible rate. cost_cv numeric(1) NULL. Coefficient variation applied drug costs simultaneously. NULL, costs held fixed. year integer(1). Budget impact year summarise. Defaults last year model. scenario character(1). Scenario use. Default "base". seed integer(1) NULL. Random seed reproducibility.

title: Run a probabilistic sensitivity analysis (PSA) — bim_run_psa
text: object class bim_psa: list elements: simulations data.frame one row per simulation: sim, budget_impact, sampled parameter values. summary data.frame mean, SD, median, 95 \ interval budget impact. year year summarised. scenario scenario used. n_sim Number simulations run. base_bi Base-case budget impact reference.

title: Run a probabilistic sensitivity analysis (PSA) — bim_run_psa
code: pop <- bim_population(   indication  = "Disease X", country = "custom",   years = 1:5, prevalence = 0.003, n_total_pop = 42e6,   diagnosed_rate = 0.60, treated_rate = 0.45, eligible_rate = 0.30 ) ms <- bim_market_share(   population     = pop,   treatments     = c("Drug C (SoC)", "Drug A (new)"),   new_drug       = "Drug A (new)",   shares_current = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 1.0, "Drug A (new)" = 0.0),   shares_new     = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 0.8, "Drug A (new)" = 0.2) ) costs <- bim_costs(   treatments = c("Drug C (SoC)", "Drug A (new)"),   drug_costs = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 500, "Drug A (new)" = 25000) ) model <- bim_model(pop, ms, costs) set.seed(1) psa <- bim_run_psa(model, n_sim = 200L, prevalence_se = 0.0005,                    eligible_rate_se = 0.05, cost_cv = 0.10) print(psa) #> htaBIM Probabilistic Sensitivity Analysis #>   Year: 5 | Scenario: base | Simulations: 200 / 200 converged #>   Base-case budget impact: GBP 50,004,500 #>  #>   PSA summary (budget impact): #>     Mean:   GBP 50,289,917 #>     Median: GBP 48,436,316 #>     SD:     GBP 12,182,224 #>     95% CrI: GBP 32,535,003  to  GBP 73,741,327

what: Cross-scenario budget impact comparison table — bim_scenario_table
title: Cross-scenario budget impact comparison table — bim_scenario_table
text: Produces side--side summary table budget impact results across scenarios model, showing Year 1, mid-point, final year, cumulative totals. standard tabular format dossier submissions following ISPOR Task Force guidelines.

title: Cross-scenario budget impact comparison table — bim_scenario_table
code: bim_scenario_table(model, years = NULL, currency_millions = TRUE, digits = 2L)

title: Cross-scenario budget impact comparison table — bim_scenario_table
text: model bim_model object. years integer vector. Years include columns. NULL (default), uses Year 1, middle year, last year. currency_millions logical(1). Express values millions. Default TRUE. digits integer(1). Decimal places formatted values. Default 2L.

title: Cross-scenario budget impact comparison table — bim_scenario_table
text: data.frame one row per scenario columns selected year plus cumulative total, formatted character strings. data.frame carries "caption" attribute suitable passing knitr::kable().

title: Cross-scenario budget impact comparison table — bim_scenario_table
code: pop <- bim_population(   indication  = "Disease X", country = "custom",   years = 1:5, prevalence = 0.003, n_total_pop = 42e6,   diagnosed_rate = 0.60, treated_rate = 0.45, eligible_rate = 0.30 ) ms <- bim_market_share(   population     = pop,   treatments     = c("Drug C (SoC)", "Drug A (new)"),   new_drug       = "Drug A (new)",   shares_current = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 1.0, "Drug A (new)" = 0.0),   shares_new     = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 0.8, "Drug A (new)" = 0.2),   scenarios      = list(     conservative = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 0.9, "Drug A (new)" = 0.1),     optimistic   = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 0.7, "Drug A (new)" = 0.3)   ) ) costs <- bim_costs(   treatments = c("Drug C (SoC)", "Drug A (new)"),   drug_costs = c("Drug C (SoC)" = 500, "Drug A (new)" = 25000) ) model <- bim_model(pop, ms, costs) st <- bim_scenario_table(model) print(st) #>       Scenario Year 1 (GBP millions) Year 3 (GBP millions) #> 1         Base                 50.00                 50.00 #> 2 Conservative                 25.01                 25.01 #> 3   Optimistic                 75.02                 75.02 #>   Year 5 (GBP millions) Cumulative (GBP millions) #> 1                 50.00                    250.02 #> 2                 25.01                    125.07 #> 3                 75.02                    375.10

title: Specify a deterministic sensitivity analysis for a budget impact model — bim_sensitivity_spec
text: Defines parameter ranges deterministic sensitivity analysis (DSA) bim_model(). parameter varied individually low high value others held base case value.

title: Specify a deterministic sensitivity analysis for a budget impact model — bim_sensitivity_spec
code: bim_sensitivity_spec(   prevalence_range = NULL,   diagnosed_rate_range = NULL,   treated_rate_range = NULL,   eligible_rate_range = NULL,   new_drug_share_range = NULL,   drug_cost_multiplier_range = c(0.85, 1.15),   extra_params = NULL )

title: Specify a deterministic sensitivity analysis for a budget impact model — bim_sensitivity_spec
text: prevalence_range numeric(2) NULL. Low high values disease prevalence (proportion). diagnosed_rate_range numeric(2) NULL. Low high values diagnosed rate. treated_rate_range numeric(2) NULL. Low high values treated rate. eligible_rate_range numeric(2) NULL. Low high values eligible rate. new_drug_share_range numeric(2) NULL. Low high values new drug market share (applied uniformly across years). drug_cost_multiplier_range numeric(2) NULL. Low high multipliers applied new drug cost (e.g. c(0.85, 1.15) plus/minus 15%). Default c(0.85, 1.15). extra_params Named list NULL. Additional parameter ranges named elements, list(label, base, low, high).

title: Generate a formatted budget impact summary table — bim_table
code: pop <- bim_population(   indication    = "Example",   country       = "GB",   years         = 1:3,   prevalence    = 0.003,   n_total_pop   = 42e6,   eligible_rate = 0.30 ) ms <- bim_market_share(   population     = pop,   treatments     = c("RASi", "NewDrug"),   new_drug       = "NewDrug",   shares_current = c(RASi = 1.0, NewDrug = 0.0),   shares_new     = c(RASi = 0.8, NewDrug = 0.2) ) costs <- bim_costs(   treatments = c("RASi", "NewDrug"),   drug_costs = c(RASi = 500, NewDrug = 25000) ) model <- bim_model(pop, ms, costs) tab <- bim_table(model) print(tab) #> $annual #>   Year Budget (current) Budget (with drug)   Budget impact Impact (%) #> 1    1   GBP 18,900,000    GBP 204,120,000 GBP 185,220,000     980.0% #> 2    2   GBP 18,900,000    GBP 204,120,000 GBP 185,220,000     980.0% #> 3    3   GBP 18,900,000    GBP 204,120,000 GBP 185,220,000     980.0% #>   Eligible patients #> 1            37,800 #> 2            37,800 #> 3            37,800 #>  #> $cumulative #>                             Metric           Value #> cum_yr1 Cumulative impact (Year 1) GBP 185,220,000 #> cum_yr2 Cumulative impact (Year 2) GBP 370,440,000 #> cum_yr3 Cumulative impact (Year 3) GBP 555,660,000 #>                   Total cumulative GBP 555,660,000 #>

what: htaBIM: Budget Impact Modelling for Health Technology Assessment — htaBIM-package
title: htaBIM: Budget Impact Modelling for Health Technology Assessment — htaBIM-package
text: htaBIM package implements structured, reproducible framework budget impact modelling (BIM) health technology assessment (HTA), following ISPOR Task Force guidelines.

title: htaBIM: Budget Impact Modelling for Health Technology Assessment — htaBIM-package
text: complete htaBIM analysis follows five steps: Population – estimate annual eligible patient population using bim_population() Market share – specify treatment shares without new drug using bim_market_share() Costs – build per-patient annual costs treatment category using bim_costs() Model – assemble run BIM using bim_model() Outputs – extract tables, plots, reports using bim_table(), plot.bim_model(), bim_report()

title: htaBIM: Budget Impact Modelling for Health Technology Assessment — htaBIM-package
text: Sullivan SD, Mauskopf JA, Augustovski F et al. (2014). Budget impact analysis–principles good practice: report ISPOR 2012 Budget Impact Analysis Good Practice II Task Force. Value Health, 17(1):5-14. doi:10.1016/j.jval.2013.08.2291 Mauskopf JA, Sullivan SD, Annemans L et al. (2007). Principles good practice budget impact analysis. Value Health, 10(5):336-347. doi:10.1111/j.1524-4733.2007.00187.x

title: htaBIM 0.1.0
text: bim_population() — epidemiology-driven patient population estimation prevalent, incident, combined approaches. bim_market_share() — treatment market share modelling constant, linear, logistic, step uptake dynamics. bim_costs() — per-patient annual cost inputs across drug, administration, monitoring, adverse events, categories. bim_costs_drug() — helper derive drug costs pack price dosing. bim_costs_ae() — helper compute adverse event costs rates. bim_model() — assembles runs complete budget impact model. bim_payer(), bim_payer_default(), bim_payer_nhs(), bim_payer_cadth(), bim_payer_us_commercial() — payer perspective tools. bim_sensitivity_spec() — specify DSA parameter ranges. bim_run_dsa() — run deterministic sensitivity analysis. bim_extract() — extract tidy results model. bim_table() — formatted budget impact summary table. bim_report() — generate text HTML report. plot.bim_model() — dispatch "line", "bar", "shares", "tornado". bim_plot_line(), bim_plot_bar(), bim_plot_shares(), bim_plot_tornado() — individual plot functions. launch_shiny() — launches interactive Shiny dashboard.
